channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Adapt to Thrive! Adaptive Power-Mean Policy Optimization for Improved LLM Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2605.04066v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) is an essential paradigm that enhances the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). However, existing methods typically rely on static policy optimization schemes that misalign with ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, LLM, arxiv, experiment, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: FMI_SU_Yotkova_Kastreva at SemEval-2026 Task 13: Lightweight Detection of LLM-Generated Code via Stylometric Signals
item.description: arXiv:2605.04157v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: SemEval-2026 Task 13 investigates machine-generated code detection across multiple programming languages and application scenarios, asking participating systems to generalize to unseen languages and domains. This paper describes our participation in S...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, LLM, arxiv, paper, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Not All That Is Fluent Is Factual: Investigating Hallucinations of Large Language Models in Academic Writing
item.description: arXiv:2605.04171v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language models (LLMs) show extraordinary abilities, but they are still prone to hallucinations, especially when we use them for generating Academic content. We have investigated four popular LLMs, ChatGPT, Grok, Gemini, and Copilot for hallucin...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompting, GPT, LLM, arxiv, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Impact of Vocabulary Overlaps on Knowledge Transfer in Multilingual Machine Translation
item.description: arXiv:2605.04196v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Knowledge transfer, especially across related languages, has been found beneficial for multilingual neural machine translation (MNMT), but some aspects are still under-explored and deserve further investigation. A joint vocabulary is most often applie...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> embedding, arxiv, experiment, paper, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Nsanku: Evaluating Zero-Shot Translation Performance of LLMs for Ghanaian Languages
item.description: arXiv:2605.04208v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated impressive multilingual capabilities for well-resourced languages, yet their performance on low-resource African languages remains poorly understood and largely unevaluated. This paper presents Nsanku, a ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, zero-shot, ICL, GPT, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Programmatic Context Augmentation for LLM-based Symbolic Regression
item.description: arXiv:2605.03101v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Symbolic regression (SR), the task of discovering mathematical expressions that best describe a given dataset, remains a fundamental challenge in scientific discovery. Traditional approaches, primarily based on genetic algorithms and related evolution...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, analysis, LLM, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Learning Correct Behavior from Examples: Validating Sequential Execution in Autonomous Agents
item.description: arXiv:2605.03159v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As autonomous agents become increasingly sophisticated, validating their sequential behavior presents a significant challenge. Traditional testing approaches require manual specification, exact sequence matching, or thousands of training examples. We ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> multimodal, example, analysis, arxiv, product | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Terminus-4B: Can a Smaller Model Replace Frontier LLMs at Agentic Execution Tasks?
item.description: arXiv:2605.03195v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern coding agents increasingly delegate specialized subtasks to subagents, which are smaller, focused agentic loops that handle narrow responsibilities like search, debugging or terminal execution. This architectural pattern keeps the main agent's ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, GPT, LLM, arxiv, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Self-Attentive Meta-Optimizer with Group-Adaptive Learning Rates and Weight Decay
item.description: arXiv:2605.04055v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Adaptive optimizers like AdamW apply uniform hyperparameters across all parameter groups, ignoring heterogeneous optimization dynamics across layers and modules. We address this limitation by proposing MetaAdamW - a new optimizer that integrates a sel...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, image, analysis, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Structured Progressive Knowledge Activation for LLM-Driven Neural Architecture Search
item.description: arXiv:2605.04057v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper focuses on a key challenge in Neural Architecture Search (NAS): integrating established architectural knowledge while exploring new designs under expensive evaluations. Large language models (LLMs) are a promising assistant for NAS because ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, arxiv, vision, paper, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MP-ISMoE: Mixed-Precision Interactive Side Mixture-of-Experts for Efficient Transfer Learning
item.description: arXiv:2605.04058v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Parameter-efficient transfer learning (PETL) has emerged as a pivotal paradigm for adapting pre-trained foundation models to downstream tasks, significantly reducing trainable parameters yet suffering from substantial memory overhead caused by gradien...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, fine-tuning, vision, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: EdgeRazor: A Lightweight Framework for Large Language Models via Mixed-Precision Quantization-Aware Distillation
item.description: arXiv:2605.04062v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent years have witnessed an increasing interest in deploying LLMs on resource-constrained devices, among which quantization has emerged as a promising lightweight technique that converts full-precision model weights and activations into lower-bit f...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> multimodal, LLM, framework, arxiv, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Computing Thiele Rules on Interval Elections and their Generalizations
item.description: arXiv:2605.03067v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Approval-based committee voting has received significant attention in the social choice community. Among the studied rules, Thiele rules, and especially Proportional Approval Voting (PAV), stand out for desirable properties such as proportional repres...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, arxiv, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Continual Distillation of Teachers from Different Domains
item.description: arXiv:2605.04059v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deep learning models continue to scale, with some requiring more storage than many large-scale datasets. Thus, we introduce a new paradigm: Continual Distillation (CD), where a student learns sequentially from a stream of teacher models without retain...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> ICL, arxiv, experiment, model, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Investigating Trustworthiness of Nonparametric Deep Survival Models for Alzheimer's Disease Progression Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2605.04063v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Alzheimer's Dementia (AD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disease marked by irreversible decline, making reliable modeling of its progression essential for effective patient care. Progression-aware methods such as survival analysis are therefore cr...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, tool, arxiv, model, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Lookahead Drifting Model
item.description: arXiv:2605.04060v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recently, a new paradigm named \emph{drifting model} has been proposed for mapping distributions, which achieves the SOTA image generation performance over ImageNet via one-step neural functional evaluation (NFE). The basic idea is to compute a drifti...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, image, arxiv, experiment, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
